text.title: message-queues Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for message-queues as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Consumer without idempotency: assumes each message is delivered exactly once.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every broker delivers at-least-once under network failure, consumer crash, or rebalancing. Without idempotency, duplicate messages produce duplicate DB rows, double-charged payments, or double-sent emails.

failure-mode.description: Using auto_ack=True (RabbitMQ) or acknowledging before processing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-ack removes the message from the queue before the handler runs. A crash mid-handler means the message is permanently lost. Use manual ack after successful processing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default acks on receipt. A worker crash after receipt but before completion silently drops the task. task_acks_late defers ack to completion, enabling safe replay.

failure-mode.description: Calling Celery apply_async() inside a DB transaction before the commit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The task fires immediately. If the transaction rolls back after the task fires, the consumer processes data that never existed. Use transaction.on_commit() or the outbox pattern.

failure-mode.description: SQS visibility timeout shorter than the p99 handler runtime.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If processing takes longer than the visibility timeout, SQS makes the message visible again while the first consumer is still processing it. Two consumers process the same message in parallel, requiring idempotency to prevent double effects.

failure-mode.description: RabbitMQ DLX/DLQ declared with mismatched arguments on different broker restarts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RabbitMQ queue arguments are immutable after declaration. A mismatch between the arguments in code and those on the broker produces a passive-declare error, silently routing to a different queue or failing to declare DLQ routing. Always test by publishing a known-bad message and checking the DLQ.

failure-mode.description: Fan-out via app code (for sub in subs: queue.publish(...)) instead of exchange/topic routing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the publisher crashes mid-loop, some subscribers receive the message and others do not. Exchange-based fan-out is atomic from the broker's perspective.
